nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving streams, shaded forest waterways, and lowland wetlands on the island of Sri Lanka.

summary: A hardy Sri Lankan crypt with narrow, wavy reddish-brown leaves. Generally considered an easy midground plant that tolerates a wide range of conditions.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne undulata is generally considered one of the more forgiving Crypts and is well suited to beginner aquascapers. It tends to do best in medium light with a nutrient-rich substrate; CO2 is not strictly required but plants have been reported to grow faster and develop richer reddish-brown coloration with light CO2 supplementation (~6-14 mg/L per Tropica). Like other Cryptocoryne species, it is prone to 'crypt melt' after relocation or sudden parameter shifts — the existing leaves dissolve but the rhizome typically regrows within a few weeks if left undisturbed. Sources disagreed on growth rate (Tropica calls it medium; FishLaboratory leans slow; some hobby sites describe it as rather quick once established) — treated here as medium. Note: there is taxonomic flux around this species — some authorities lump it under Cryptocoryne crispatula var. undulata, and historical synonyms include C. axelrodii and C. willisii var. undulata. The Tropica 'Broad Leaf' cultivar is a triploid form of the same species. Adult height has been reported anywhere from about 15 cm to over 30 cm depending on light, CO2, and substrate.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory
additional.notes: Designated Buce Plant secondary was swapped — Buce Plant catalog did not surface a Cryptocoryne undulata product page. Fish Laboratory used to cross-check temperature (22-28°C), pH (5.5-7.5), hardness (1-15 dGH), family (Araceae), and historical synonyms (C. axelrodii, C. willisii var. undulata).

additional.name: Aquatics Pool Spa
additional.notes: Third source used to confirm temperature 68-83°F (20-28°C), pH 6.5-7.0, low-to-moderate light, CO2 not required, propagation via runners. Disagrees with FishLab on growth rate (calls it 'rather quick' vs. FishLab's slow); averaged to medium.
